description: Free-text notes attached to any entity in any module. Enables collaboration — a buyer can note why a PO was delayed, an approver can explain a rejection, an agent can log a decision rationale. Comments are visible to all users with access to the parent entity. Modules never manage comment storage directly.
routine: Comment.add(ctx, input) / Comment.edit(ctx, input) / Comment.remove(ctx, id) / Comment.findByEntity(ctx, input)
consumers: any module — comment threads appear on POs, invoices, suppliers, MDM entities, etc.

EntityId.constraints: id of the entity this comment belongs to

ParentCommentId.constraints: id of the parent comment when this is a reply. Null for top-level comments. Max nesting depth: 1 (replies to replies are not allowed)

Text.constraints: comment body. Max 4000 chars. Plain text — no HTML. Stored in DynamoDB only to keep PostgreSQL index lean

AuthorType.constraints: 'system' for automated comments added by routines, e.g. 'Status changed to Approved by approval workflow'

Module.constraints: module that created the comment — for filtering and access control

IsSystemGenerated.constraints: true when added automatically by a routine rather than a human. System comments are displayed differently in the UI

EditedAt.constraints: set when the comment text is updated. Null if never edited. Only the author may edit within 15 minutes of creation

DeletedAt.constraints: soft delete — deleted comments show as '[removed]' in the thread to preserve reply context

CreatedAt.constraints: set by MDM routine at write time

postgresql.note: primary query — full comment thread for an entity in order
fetch replies for a top-level comment
what did this user comment on

dynamodb.note: full comment record including Text — PostgreSQL index has no Text to stay lean

consistencyContract.note: Text is written to DynamoDB in the same request — comment must be readable immediately after creation

retention.postgresql: permanent while entity exists — soft delete preserves thread structure

persistence.note: Text and EditedAt are mutable within the edit window. DeletedAt is set on soft delete. No other fields change after creation
